branch_note: Resumed system-redesign buildout branch (PR #195). Wave 8.8 checkpoint complete: the inspectable Memory Center and Financial Operating Profile are owner-scoped, persisted, wired into deterministic concentration-run defaults with snapshotted provenance, and locally plus hosted-RLS verified. Tsc is clean, lint has 0 errors / 0 warnings, 792 tests pass, the authenticated browser suite passes 4/4, production build succeeds, all 176 route budgets pass, and total static JS is 3919/4400 KB.

rules: ship complete vertical slices, not prototypes
preserve existing behavior unless the wave changes it
no schema change without migration + RLS review

baseline_health.stack: Next.js 15 App Router, React 19, TS 5.8, Supabase, Tailwind v4, Vitest, Playwright, Sentry

phases.1_audit: substantial — repo mapped; docs/axis-redesign/01-current-state-audit.md; risk/defect ledgers. Not an exhaustive per-module audit.
phases.2_product_synthesis: substantial — 02-product-synthesis + feature matrix + IA (Operate section added). Not exhaustive.
phases.3_target_architecture: substantial — 04-target-architecture + ADRs 0001 (money), 0002 (action taxonomy/approval kernel), 0003 (durable tasks/runs). More ADRs as waves land.
phases.4_financial_domain: complete — deterministic money + multi-currency minor-unit table + FX conversion with provenance (currency.ts/fx.ts), tax lots & corporate actions (taxLots.ts), provenance populated from all write paths (manual holdings/liabilities, order ticket, Plaid txn sync + FundSpendingModule freshness badge), holdings reconciliation wired to /api/fund/holdings + indicator in the holdings table.
phases.5_security_kernel: core done — action taxonomy + prompt-injection rule, approval object, approvals persistence + isActionable-gated execute, approval-card UI. Step-up bound to a REAL WebAuthn passkey assertion; self-attestable boolean removed; adversarial review done + hardened (per-approval challenge binding, per-user rate limit) — see docs/axis-redesign/security/step-up-review.md. RESIDUAL (needs a human): independent security sign-off + manual authenticator test.
phases.6_design_system: partial — semantic status token layer consolidated (src/lib/design/statusTokens.ts; freshness/task/approval presenters route through it, colors pinned by test) + 06-design-system doc. No typography/motion/surface overhaul (existing AXIS system retained).
phases.7_workspace_shell: started — command system extended: new Operate routes are palette-covered (guard-tested) and an executable 'Run Concentration Check' run-routine command added. Split-panes / entity-references / hover-previews not attempted.
phases.8_core_modules: substantial — Tasks + Approvals workbenches shipped; Portfolio has pure tested TWR/MWR/allocation math, Investing allocation/concentration views, and Fund provider connection health/provenance. Activity has deterministic categorization/anomaly rules; Research persists cited owner-scoped reports; Signals preserves an honest empty inbox. The Memory Center now exposes owner-scoped inspectable context and a confirmed Financial Operating Profile whose concentration limit feeds deterministic routine defaults with immutable provenance. Need-to-Know expansion and additional module depth remain.
phases.9_agent_runtime: complete for the current redesign scope — durable routine runs + step snapshots, deterministic routines, approval-gated resume that replays completed step outputs without recomputing, explain-only LLM narration with cost metering, observability events, run-history detail, and routine version compare/clone/restore. Conversational routine builder and subagent execution remain future program work.
phases.10_integrations: substantial — integration risk model + provider->domain adapters behind the shared Result contract: market-data (Polygon), Plaid accounts/transactions/liabilities, Public order boundary, webhook hardening, bounded read retries, widget stale-cache fallback, Control Room risk cards, Make notifications/outbox, and cache-first Mail across direct/Composio transports. Make management list/detail/run/start/stop are schema-validated Results with read-only retry/write-once semantics. Mail now serves owner-scoped header/snippet cache rows before explicit provider revalidation, preserves failed-account rows, and records per-account sync state without caching bodies. The available active Composio Gmail account passed live list/detail adapter validation without exposing content; direct Gmail and both Outlook transports remain unverified because no active hosted connections exist.
phases.11_performance: in progress — CI enforces BOTH a total static-JS bundle budget (check-bundle-budget.mjs, from #191) and per-route first-load budgets (check-perf-budgets.mjs). Wave 11.3 code-split Command from 433kB to 361kB; wave 11.4 code-split Vitality from 391kB to 361kB; wave 11.5 code-split Mail from 388kB to 361kB after wave 10.16 removed provider fan-out from first paint; wave 11.6 code-split Dispatch from 378kB to 362kB. Further route profiling remains.
phases.12_testing: ongoing — 792 unit tests / 124 files incl. financial invariants, provider adapter contracts, encrypted outbox/tamper/dedupe/replay behavior, Make management safety, Mail cache privacy/auth/ownership/sync behavior, portfolio math, Activity rules, cited research provenance, memory/profile contracts and APIs, and concentration-profile provenance. The seeded local authenticated browser suite passes all 4 Mail/daily-route/redirect/memory-lifecycle workflows; public CI smoke remains. Broader provider-backed and visual-regression coverage remains.
phases.13_observability: started — structured API error codes + a redacted structured-event helper (src/lib/observability/events.ts) with a tested secret/PII redaction guard, emitting safe run.completed/run.failed events from the concentration routine. No new Sentry dashboards.
phases.14_cicd: core done — .github/workflows/ci.yml gates lint + typecheck + unit tests + build on every PR (program §20). Local Docker migration verification is restored and the hosted Supabase migration/read-back gate is active. The daily cron now uses service-role RPC access; public/anon/authenticated execution of expire_stale_approvals is revoked. Deployment/rollback strategy is otherwise unchanged.

convergence_note: This program is NOT a completed 14-phase rewrite — the initial prompt describes a multi-quarter effort. What is delivered is a coherent, green, end-to-end vertical of the operating loop (observe->task->approval->durable run) on a deterministic financial-safety kernel, plus foundational domain primitives, all additive and behind existing behavior. Remaining phases are honestly tracked above and in next_dependency_ordered_actions. No delivered wave is a broken prototype; every one is tsc/lint/test green and the migrations are applied+verified.

waves.title: Security kernel — action taxonomy + approval policy (pure)
waves.note: ActionClass (READ..DESTRUCTIVE_ADMIN), BASE_REQUIREMENT, decideApproval/requiresApproval. Enforces the combinatorial prompt-injection rule (sensitive data + untrusted external content + outbound/execution => mandatory approval) and forbids downgrading financial execution / destructive admin. Pure; the approval-object persistence + UI cards are later waves.

waves.title: Approval object — §11.3 durable approval contract (pure)
waves.note: ApprovalRequest shape + buildApprovalRequest (derives requirement/reasons/step-up from decideApproval; forces one_time scope for FINANCIAL_EXECUTION/DESTRUCTIVE_ADMIN), validateApprovalCompleteness ('never a bare Allow' — outbound/executing need freshness+expiry; execution needs amount/account/before+after; destructive needs before-state), isApprovalExpired (fail-safe: unparseable => expired), isActionable (single execution gate: complete + unexpired + step-up satisfied). Pure; this is the typed contract the drafted approvals table + future approval-card UI implement.

Tokenized, accessible FreshnessBadge primitive (role=status, aria-label) deriving tier via classifyFreshness. waves.title: Approvals persistence (isActionable-gated execute path)
waves.note: Persists the §11.3 approval object. Pure approvalPersistence maps ApprovalRequest<->row (round-trip tested). POST /api/approvals builds via buildApprovalRequest and REJECTS auto (nothing to approve) or incomplete (never a bare Allow, returns missing[]). PATCH /api/approvals/[id] approve/deny (pending-only; expired pending -> expired) and execute — the safety gate: reconstructs the request from the STORED row (never client-supplied) and re-runs isActionable (complete + unexpired + step-up satisfied) before clearing to 'executed'. Records the gate passing only; the side-effecting action is intentionally NOT performed (no autonomous execution, §7/§11.2).
waves.tests: 508 tests pass (+3); tsc clean; lint clean
waves.verification: npx tsc --noEmit + npx eslint src/app/api/approvals + npm run test

waves.title: Approval-card UI + approvals queue (user-facing, §11.3)
waves.note: User-facing approval queue on /approvals (Operate section, status=beta). ApprovalCard renders the FULL §11.3 scope — action class, exact summary, tool, target, amount, before/after, data freshness (FreshnessBadge), requested-by, expiry, and the reasons — never a bare Allow. Approve/Deny for pending; step-up classes gate Approve behind an explicit step-up confirmation checkbox; 'Clear to execute' for approved (hits the isActionable-gated PATCH). Pure approvalCardView (labels/tones/amount/status) unit-tested. All states handled. Empty until agents/routines create approvals (honest empty state — no fabricated rows). next build compiles.
waves.tests: 513 tests pass (+5); tsc clean; lint clean; next build compiled successfully
waves.verification: npx tsc --noEmit + npx eslint + npm run test + npm run build (compile)

waves.title: First deterministic routine — concentration check creates real Tasks
waves.note: Closes the observe->significance->task arc with deterministic math (no LLM, no fabricated data). Pure reviewConcentration(positions, maxWeight) computes cent-exact weights, flags positions over target, and returns the trim amount + a stable breachObjective (idempotency key). POST /api/routines/concentration-check reads the user's real fund_holdings, runs the skill, and creates a 'queued' agent-Task per breach — idempotent (skips breaches whose objective already has a non-terminal task). It never trades or creates a financial-execution approval; output is a task to review. 'Run concentration check' button on the Tasks workbench. Approvals queue intentionally stays empty until a real execution skill with order details exists — no fabricated trade approvals.
waves.tests: 519 tests pass (+6); tsc clean; lint clean
waves.verification: npx tsc --noEmit + npx eslint + npm run test

waves.title: Durable routine-run execution (§15.5) — run + step snapshots
waves.note: Durable execution backbone. Pure runState (RunStatus/StepStatus + transitions + deriveRunOutcome: all-ok=completed, mixed=partial, all-fail=failed, empty=completed). Migration routine_runs + routine_step_runs (owner-scoped RLS, no-delete audit, applied+verified: 3 policies each). Concentration routine refactored to open a durable run and record each step (load_holdings, review_concentration, create_tasks) with input/output snapshots, deriving the final run status; a thrown step marks the run failed with snapshots intact. GET /api/routines/runs (list + ?runId detail with steps). RoutineRunsPanel shows recent runs on the Tasks workbench (honest empty until a run exists). Cost fields recorded (0 — deterministic, no model).
waves.tests: 524 tests pass (+5); tsc clean; lint clean; next build compiles
waves.verification: npx tsc --noEmit + npx eslint + npm run test + npm run build + live RLS policy read-back

Makes the AGENTS.md minimum bar a real merge gate instead of the once-daily health check. Build step also prints route bundle sizes for §11.

waves.title: Integration risk model — capabilities -> action classes (§16)
waves.note: Pure model composing the security kernel: capabilityActionClass (list/read->READ, markRead/archive->INTERNAL_WRITE, send/reply->EXTERNAL_COMMUNICATION, delete->DESTRUCTIVE_ADMIN), capabilitiesRiskProfile/providerRiskProfile -> {read/write/send/destructive caps, highestClass, riskLevel, approvalDefault (from decideApproval), enabledCount}. Lets Control Room organize integrations by capability+risk, and routines inherit approval defaults. Disabled caps never contribute risk.

waves.title: Bind approval step-up to a real WebAuthn passkey assertion (§11.2)
waves.note: SECURITY FIX + feature. Previously the approve path trusted a client-supplied stepUpVerified boolean (self-attestable). Now step_up_verified_at is set ONLY by /api/approvals/[id]/step-up after a verified passkey assertion by the approval's OWNER (credential must belong to the user; one-time challenge bound to user_id; reuses the verified src/lib/webauthn/server helpers). Removed the boolean from PATCH. ApprovalCard replaces the checkbox with a 'Verify with passkey' ceremony; Approve for step-up classes is gated on step_up_verified_at. No schema change (uses existing approvals column + user_passkeys/webauthn_challenges). Reuses the exact pattern of the login passkey route. MUST get human security review + a manual test with a real authenticator before production.
waves.tests: 539 tests pass; tsc clean; lint clean; next build compiles. WebAuthn ceremony NOT unit-testable here (no authenticator).
waves.verification: npx tsc --noEmit + npx eslint + npm run test + npm run build; manual authenticator test pending

waves.title: Market-data provider adapter (§10 provider->domain boundary)
waves.note: Stable MarketDataAdapter contract (getQuote -> Result<MarketQuote>) with a Polygon/Massive implementation over the existing rate-limited client. Pure normalizeQuote stamps provenance (provider/retrievedAt/effectiveAt/currency) and classifies freshness against FRESHNESS_SLAS.marketPrice — so a delayed feed reads as delayed/stale, never 'live'. Errors map to the shared IntegrationError taxonomy (not_found/rate_limited/provider_error via failFromException). /api/markets/quote returns the normalized domain quote (feeds FreshnessBadge). Call sites never branch on provider.
waves.tests: 544 tests pass (+5); tsc clean; lint clean
waves.verification: npx tsc --noEmit + npx eslint + npm run test

waves.title: Plaid account adapter — balances into the provenance model (§10)
waves.note: AccountAdapter contract (getAccounts(userId) -> Result<Account[]>) with a Plaid impl. Pure normalizeAccount(s) maps Plaid balances to a domain Account with provenance (provider/retrievedAt/currency) + freshness (accountBalance SLA) — balances read as fresh right after a live pull. Self-contained in lib (creds via env, reuses getPlaidAccessToken; token never leaves the server). Errors -> IntegrationError taxonomy; not_supported/auth_expired return a soft connect-a-bank state (200), harder failures map to status. /api/plaid/accounts uses it; existing /api/plaid/balances untouched.
waves.tests: 549 tests pass (+5); tsc clean; lint clean
waves.verification: npx tsc --noEmit + npx eslint + npm run test

waves.title: Step-up security review + hardening
waves.note: Adversarial review of the step-up flow (IDOR, foreign credential, replay, confused-deputy, brute force, user-verification, counter, leakage) — see the review doc. Two hardenings applied: (1) challenge now carries approval_id and verify requires it to match THIS approval (migration 202607140200, applied+verified); (2) per-user rate limit (10/10min) on verify, matching the login passkey route. Findings 1-4,7,8,10 already safe. Residual (human-only): independent sign-off + manual authenticator test.
waves.tests: 549 tests pass; tsc clean; lint clean
waves.verification: npx tsc --noEmit + npx eslint + npm run test; live migration verified

waves.title: Step-up freshness window (follow-up PR, post-merge)
waves.note: A step-up verification no longer stays valid indefinitely. isActionable now takes stepUpVerifiedAt (timestamp) not a boolean and requires it within STEP_UP_MAX_AGE_MS (5 min) via the pure isStepUpFresh (fail-safe on missing/unparseable/future). Execute path distinguishes STEP_UP_REQUIRED vs STEP_UP_STALE (409); the card re-shows 'Verify with passkey' when stale and the queue toasts a re-verify prompt. Also verified the #190 production deploy: Vercel READY on ca73d37 + no new Sentry prod errors (24h).
waves.tests: 563 tests pass; tsc clean; lint clean
waves.verification: npx tsc --noEmit + npx eslint + npm run test

waves.title: Provenance on persisted holdings + freshness badge on Assets (follow-up)
waves.note: Manual holdings POST now stamps provider/retrieved_at/currency; GET returns retrieved_at (oldest across sources = conservative aggregate freshness). FundNetWorthModule Assets card shows a per-holding FreshnessBadge (holdings SLA: 12h fresh / 48h stale). Existing rows have null retrieved_at -> reads 'unknown' honestly until re-synced; new/updated holdings read fresh and age. Added the provenance columns (provider/retrieved_at/effective_at/currency/reconciliation_state/provider_record_id) to database.types.ts fund_holdings (were applied to the DB in the 202607131700 migration but never typed).

waves.title: Resumable durable execution + idempotency (§15.5, follow-up)
waves.note: Pure planResume(orderedKeys, existingSteps) -> {toRun, reuse}: succeeded steps are skipped and their output snapshots reused (idempotent replay); order preserved; a key counts done if any record succeeded. isStepAlreadyDone/isRunComplete helpers. Concentration routine now RESUMABLE: POST {runId} resumes a non-terminal run, skipping succeeded steps and reusing their outputs; a step failure now marks the run 'blocked' (non-terminal, resumable) instead of terminal 'failed', emitting routine.run.blocked. The pausing-for-approval routine that fully exercises resume is the next adopter (the framework + storage support it today).
waves.tests: 569 tests pass (+6); tsc clean; lint clean; next build compiles
waves.verification: npx tsc --noEmit + npx eslint + npm run test + npm run build

waves.title: Approval-pause routine executor + resume endpoint (§15.5, follow-up)
waves.note: Generalized the inline concentration-check step runner into a reusable durable executor. Steps can return pauseForApproval(approvalId,idempotencyKey), leaving the paused step running and moving routine_runs to waiting_for_approval with paused_step_key/approval_id/idempotency_key. POST /api/routines/runs/[id]/resume owner-checks the run, verifies the stored approval row through rowToApprovalRequest + isActionable, consumes the approval, replays succeeded routine_step_runs.output_snapshot rows without recomputing, and continues from the paused step. The concentration routine now uses the executor while preserving existing behavior; unsupported routine keys are rejected before approval consumption.

waves.title: Routine run-history detail view (§15.5, follow-up)
waves.note: RoutineRunsPanel now opens the existing /api/routines/runs?runId= detail API and renders the persisted run audit trail: routine version/trigger/status, output summary, step timeline, step errors, truncated input/output snapshots, pause metadata, and a resume action for approval-waiting runs. Loading/error/detail states are visible; run list failures no longer silently disappear. Pure presenter helpers route status colors through semantic status tokens and summarize known output shapes without fabricating metrics.

waves.title: Routine versioning compare / clone / restore (§15.5, follow-up)
waves.note: Adds code-defined built-in routine versions plus user-owned routine_versions snapshots. APIs: GET /api/routines/versions lists built-ins + user versions; POST /api/routines/versions clones a source version as a draft; GET/PATCH /api/routines/versions/[id] reads and restores a user version by cloning it as active while archiving the previous active version; POST /api/routines/versions/compare returns a deterministic diff across steps, inputs, and safety contracts. Restore/clone are persistence actions only: they do not execute routines or financial actions.

waves.title: Structured Make management API boundary
waves.note: Removes the remaining MakeError/throw boundary from list/detail/run/start/stop and team resolution. Every management operation now returns the shared Result contract, validates zone/team/scenario ids before fetch, rejects redirects, schema-validates 2xx payloads, records safe normalized provider failures, and never surfaces response bodies or thrown messages. GET reads use at most three bounded attempts; run/start/stop writes use exactly one. Scenario execution now sends Make's documented `data` field instead of the ineffective legacy `input` field, rejects non-serializable input, and returns a validated execution receipt. Activation/deactivation returns and verifies Make's id/state confirmation. Low-level writes remain authority-free and require a future authenticated caller to satisfy Axis action policy. Configured Make list/detail reads passed live shape checks without printing scenario names or payloads; no live write was performed.

waves.title: Cache-first Mail synchronization across direct and Composio transports
waves.note: Separates Mail first paint from live provider fan-out. GET /api/mail/inbox is now an owner-scoped cache-only read; the workspace renders saved normalized list metadata and explicitly POSTs /api/mail/sync to revalidate direct Gmail, direct Outlook, Composio Gmail, and Composio Outlook through the existing adapter Result contract. The cache stores sender/subject/snippet/flags/dates only, never bodies, attachments, tokens, raw payloads, or provider error bodies. Per-account sync state keeps last success plus normalized failure codes. Successful first-page generations reconcile only their covered date window; pagination appends; failed accounts retain and visibly surface last-known rows. Provider actions reconcile cached unread/removal state and return a visible warning if only the secondary cache update fails. Supabase is the store; Tembo remains unused pending a role decision.

waves.title: Inspectable Memory Center and Financial Operating Profile
waves.note: Adds a user-inspectable context store and one confirmed financial operating profile per owner. Memory records bounded kind/scope/content, immutable provenance, confidence, expiry, and archive lifecycle; authenticated hard delete and anonymous access are denied. Strict APIs and the /memory workspace cover list/create/edit/archive/restore plus profile read/update with visible loading, empty, signed-out, failure, confirmation, busy, and success states. Memory is explicitly context only and cannot satisfy approval, step-up, or execution authority. New concentration runs resolve a valid explicit limit, then the profile's integer-basis-point limit, then the legacy default, and snapshot the selected value plus provenance for deterministic resume.

migrations_applied.defect_TASK_collision: CAUGHT PRE-APPLY: public.tasks already exists as the user's to-do/Agenda table. Drafted migration used create-table-if-not-exists public.tasks, which would have silently no-opped and left approvals.task_id FK pointing at to-do rows. Fixed by namespacing the durable agent-Task tables to agent_tasks / agent_task_activity and repointing approvals.task_id -> agent_tasks(id).
